title: sericocarpus oregonensis var. oregonensis
intro: Sericocarpus oregonensis var. oregonensis, commonly known as Oregon woolly aster, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that grows in the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Oregon woolly aster are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in creams, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically to alleviate symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin conditions.
